text.title: Roadmap Drift Detection Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every weekly roadmap-drift check must enforce. These are HARD rules — a softer review is not a drift check, it is a status update.

rule.statement: The drift check runs on a fixed weekly cadence (same weekday, same window) for as long as the roadmap is published. Skipped cycles are recorded as "skipped: reason" — never silently dropped.
rule.rationale: Drift detection only works if the sampling interval is constant. Irregular cadence makes deltas non-comparable across weeks and lets the PM unconsciously avoid checking when things are slipping.

rule.statement: Each cycle scores all five axes — scope (committed vs shipped), schedule (milestone date delta), confidence (band shift), dependency (blocker status delta), narrative (what the team now says vs what the roadmap still says). A check that omits any axis is rejected as incomplete.
rule.rationale: Single-axis status (e.g. "we are on schedule") hides drift on other axes. The narrative axis specifically catches the silent-killer case where the team has informally written off a milestone but the roadmap still shows it as committed.

rule.statement: Every non-zero delta names a single accountable owner (handle or org role) and a next-action verb. Plural ownership ("the team will look at it") is rejected; "owner: pending" is allowed for exactly one cycle then auto-escalates.
rule.rationale: Without a named owner a drift item is just a complaint. The one-cycle "pending" allowance lets the PM file the delta the moment it is observed without waiting for an owner conversation; the auto-escalation prevents indefinite parking.

rule.statement: Confidence deltas are recorded with a sign and a magnitude band (e.g. "-1 band: high→medium"), not as free-form prose. Unsigned or unbanded confidence change is rejected.
rule.rationale: Free-form confidence language ("we feel slightly less sure") cannot be trended, charted, or compared cycle-over-cycle. Signed band deltas turn confidence into a tracked metric and surface the case where confidence quietly degrades for 3 weeks before any date moves.

rule.statement: Any of the following triggers an out-of-band stakeholder note within 24h, not "we will mention it next review": scope-delta >15% of cycle scope, schedule-delta >1 sprint on any committed milestone, confidence drop of 2+ bands, or 2 consecutive cycles of same-direction drift on the same axis.
rule.rationale: Roadmap credibility dies from surprise, not from bad news. Hard thresholds remove PM discretion to "wait and see" — the same drift that erodes trust when surfaced late builds trust when surfaced early.

example.description: Cycle output for week 2026-W19, project "Pricing v2".
example.code: # Drift Record W19 - cycle: 2026-W19 (Monday pulse) - previous: 2026-W18 - axes: - scope: committed=8 items, shipped=6, delta=-25% → ESCALATE (rule r5) - schedule: "Beta open" planned W20 → now W21, delta=+1 sprint → ESCALATE - confidence: high → medium, signed_delta=-1 band - dependency: legal review still blocking, no change (2nd cycle same) → ESCALATE - narrative: team standup says "beta is now a feature flag, not a launch" — roadmap still says "public beta launch" → MISMATCH - owners: - scope: @pm (talk to design lead about over-commit) - schedule: @eng-lead (re-baseline W21) - dependency: @pm (escalate to legal) - next_check: 2026-W20 Monday 10:00
